items.title: Define launcher cleanup hooks for DevNexus runner safety
items.description:
Type: cleanup-boundary implementation/planning slice
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready (AFK) for hook mapping; human-in-the-loop (HITL) before enabling host launch smokes

Goal: identify launcher cleanup and status hooks needed before DevNexus or a coordinator agent can safely authorize host process or image launch smokes.

Acceptance criteria:
- Required status, stop, timeout, and cleanup signals are documented or exposed through existing launcher-safe surfaces.
- The slice distinguishes read-only inspection from host process mutation.
- Any missing cleanup hooks are recorded as follow-up work instead of bypassing safety gates.
- No live image or host process launch is performed while mapping the boundary.

Verification:
- Run focused tests or static checks that do not require live image startup.
- Confirm existing ahead-of-origin commits are preserved and not rewritten.

items.title: Confirm launcher cleanup/status hooks for approved runner harness
items.description:
Type: launcher cleanup follow-up slice
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready (AFK) after the approved runner harness identifies exact launcher hook needs; human-in-the-loop (HITL) for any live host mutation.
Source plan: PLAN.md priority candidate 9.

Goal: make any launcher-side status, stop, timeout, or cleanup hooks required by the approved isolated runner explicit and tested inside the launcher boundary.

Acceptance criteria:
- Required launcher-owned status and cleanup signals are documented or exposed through launcher-safe MCP surfaces.
- Read-only inspection is clearly separated from host process or image mutation.
- Missing hooks discovered by the approved runner are implemented or recorded as narrower follow-ups.
- No DevNexus, DevNexus-Pharo, PLexus policy, or project-specific terminology is introduced into launcher-owned code.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Depends on the approved runner harness naming a concrete launcher hook gap.
- Do not launch images or mutate host processes without explicit runner approval.

Verification:
- Run focused launcher tests/static checks that do not require live image startup.
- If live checks are later approved, run only through the approved runner and record cleanup evidence.

Publication notes:
- Direct integration to origin/main after launcher-safe tests pass.

items.title: Confirm launcher image delete contract for scoped PLexus cleanup
items.description:
Type: launcher cleanup contract follow-up
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready (AFK) after PLexus identifies a concrete launcher-side gap; human-in-the-loop (HITL) for live deletion checks.
Source plan: PLAN.md Plugin Direction and scoped PLexus image lifecycle requirement.

Goal: confirm whether pharo-launcher-mcp already exposes the image deletion/status hooks needed for scoped PLexus cleanup, and implement a launcher-owned follow-up only if a concrete gap exists.

Acceptance criteria:
- The launcher-side contract for image delete/status/error reporting is documented or tested without mentioning DevNexus, DevNexus-Pharo, or PLexus policy.
- If existing launcher capabilities are sufficient, record that fact and leave implementation untouched.
- If a gap exists, expose the narrow launcher-owned hook needed by PLexus cleanup without adding project policy to the launcher.
- Live delete checks, if needed, run only through the approved isolated runner against disposable images.

Blockers and boundaries:
- Depends on plexus:local-8 or the approved runner identifying a concrete launcher hook gap.
- Do not delete images or mutate host launcher state before approval.

Verification:
- Run focused launcher tests/static checks for contract coverage.
- Run live checks only through the approved runner if later approved.

Publication notes:
- Direct integration to origin/main after launcher-safe verification passes.

items.title: Fix isolated launcher profile image copy configuration
items.description:
Type: launcher isolated profile/config follow-up
Readiness: autonomous agent-ready (AFK) for config/invocation tests and docs; human-in-the-loop (HITL) for any live image copy retry.
Source finding: approved downstream live smoke using an isolated pharo-launcher-mcp profile failed before project open while copying source image `MCP12-2` to a disposable target. With only profile env vars, Pharo Launcher raised `PrimitiveFailed: primitive #createDirectory: in WindowsStore failed`. With `PHARO_LAUNCHER_MCP_LAUNCHER_CONFIGURATION=overnight-live-20260517`, it looked for `C:\Users\gabriel.darbord\AppData\Local\Pharo Launcher\overnight-live-20260517` instead of the isolated profile root.

Goal: make image copy/create operations honor the isolated pharo-launcher-mcp profile paths, or fail early with a clear profile/config validation error before invoking Pharo Launcher.

Acceptance criteria:
- Launcher config/invocation tests cover profile env vars for image copy/create operations, including launcher image, images dir, templates dir, logs dir, and optional launcher configuration.
- If `PHARO_LAUNCHER_MCP_LAUNCHER_CONFIGURATION` is supported, it resolves to the intended profile config path or docs explain the required value and setup.
- Image copy/create does not fall back to the host WindowsStore/default image repository when an isolated profile root is configured.
- Failure messages identify missing profile/config paths before host mutation whenever possible.
- Source code and docs remain launcher-owned and do not introduce downstream project policy.

Verification:
- Focused pharo-launcher-mcp config/invocation tests.
- Static checks that do not launch images.
- Live image copy retry only through the approved isolated profile after static verification.

Publication notes:
- Direct integration to origin/main after launcher-safe verification passes.

local-1.body:
Completed in this cycle. Added docs/cleanup-hook-boundary.md and linked it from docs/development.md. The boundary distinguishes read-only planning checks from host inspection and mutation, documents status and timeout signals, maps process/image cleanup hooks, and records that no launcher-side blocking cleanup hook gap is currently identified for an isolated PLexus smoke. No live image, host process launch, process kill, image delete, package install, or integration smoke was performed.

Verification passed: git diff --check; npm run build; npm test. Existing ahead-of-origin commit 0f75151 was preserved, not rewritten, and published with new commit 1f3070b to origin/main.

local-2.body: Vibe backlog reconciliation on 2026-05-16: FCD-80 is now contained in origin/main as 0f75151 and FCD-81 appears substantially covered by current image copy verification plus lifecycle integration coverage for create -> info and copy -> info. Keep this item dependent on the approved PLexus runner identifying a concrete missing launcher hook rather than duplicating the stale Vibe items.

local-4.body:
Completed and published.

Commit: pharo-launcher-mcp 24f6d84 (`Honor isolated launcher profiles`) pushed to origin/main.

What changed:
- Isolated profile mode now defaults the launcher CLI configuration under the profile root and resolves relative configuration names under `<stateRoot>\launcher`.
- Profile invocation writes a Pharo Launcher CLI configuration that points image, VM, template, init-script, and log locations at the isolated profile.
- Copied image metadata is repaired after Pharo Launcher renames image files, so copied targets become listable/inspectable before downstream open.
- Detached image launches redirect launcher stdout/stderr into profile log files. This fixed the Windows detached launch path where ignored stdio produced no discoverable target image process.

Verification:
- `npm test` passed: 12 files, 102 tests.
- `npm run typecheck` passed.
- `npm run build` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Focused approved-profile copy/info/delete from `MCP12-2` passed with metadata repair.
- Focused approved-profile detached launch produced a target process within 1 second and cleaned only the owned disposable image/process.
- Downstream PLexus smoke `dogfood-overnight-local-3-20260517-0428` passed open/route/close using this published fix.

Follow-up publication: released @evref-bl/pharo-launcher-mcp@0.1.2 from source commit c137fe9 after npm test, typecheck, build, pack dry-run, and npm publish passed. PLexus npm-path smoke now consumes this package without a local checkout override.
